agent_output:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Critical race condition between stock check and decrement allows overselling under concurrent requests"
  },
  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "TOCTOU Race Condition: Stock Check vs. Decrement",
      "risk": "Multiple concurrent requests read the same stock value, all pass the check, and all decrement — resulting in negative stock and overselling. With popular products this is near-certain under real traffic.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "addToCart function, lines between getStock() and decrementStock()",
      "reproduction": "1. Product X has stock=1. 2. User A calls addToCart(A, X, 1) — reads stock=1, passes check. 3. Before A's decrementStock executes, User B calls addToCart(B, X, 1) — also reads stock=1, passes check. 4. Both decrement. Stock is now -1. Two users 'bought' the same unit.",
      "fix": "Use an atomic compare-and-decrement operation or a database transaction with row-level locking. For example: `UPDATE products SET stock = stock - $qty WHERE id = $productId AND stock >= $qty` and check affected rows. If 0 rows affected, throw insufficient stock. This collapses the read-check-write into a single atomic operation.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Stock integrity, order fulfillment correctness"
    },
    {
      "title": "No Atomicity Between Cart Insert and Stock Decrement",
      "risk": "If `addItem` succeeds but `decrementStock` fails (DB timeout, connection drop, process crash), the item is in the user's cart but stock was never decremented. Conversely, if the cart insert is later rolled back or the user abandons, stock is permanently lost (phantom decrement).",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "addToCart function — sequential awaits with no transaction boundary",
      "reproduction": "1. addItem succeeds. 2. decrementStock throws (e.g., DB connection reset). 3. Cart contains item, stock unchanged — user can check out against un-reserved stock. Or: reverse scenario where stock is decremented but cart insert silently fails on retry logic upstream.",
      "fix": "Wrap both operations in a single database transaction: `await db.transaction(async (tx) => { ... })`. Both succeed or both roll back. Alternatively, perform the atomic stock decrement first, and only add to cart if it succeeds — with a compensating increment on cart failure.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Data consistency between cart and inventory"
    },
    {
      "title": "No Input Validation on Public API Inputs",
      "risk": "quantity could be 0, negative, or non-integer. A negative quantity would pass the stock check (stock is almost always >= negative number) and then *increment* stock via decrementStock(-n), creating inventory out of thin air. Zero quantity wastes a DB round-trip and pollutes the cart.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "addToCart function — no validation on quantity parameter",
      "reproduction": "Call addToCart('user1', 'prod1', -5). Stock check: e.g. 10 < -5 is false → passes. decrementStock('prod1', -5) effectively adds 5 to stock. Repeat to generate unlimited stock.",
      "fix": "Add upfront validation: `if (!Number.isInteger(quantity) || quantity < 1) throw new Error('Invalid quantity');`. Also validate userId and productId are non-empty strings.",
      "time_to_fix": "5 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Security, inventory integrity"
    }
  ],
  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No idempotency protection",
      "description": "If the client retries a failed/timed-out request, the same item could be added to the cart and stock decremented multiple times. Consider an idempotency key or upsert semantics on the cart.",
      "location": "addToCart function"
    }
  ],
  "suggestions": [
    "Consider using SELECT ... FOR UPDATE or an equivalent row lock if your DB supports it, to serialize concurrent access per-product",
    "Add a stock reservation pattern (soft-hold with TTL) instead of immediate decrement, so abandoned carts auto-release inventory",
    "Rate-limit this endpoint per-user to reduce thundering-herd pressure on popular products"
  ],
  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "Current code will oversell popular products under any meaningful concurrent load. This is not a theoretical risk — it is the expected behavior.",
    "rollback_capability": "N/A — standalone function, rollback is straightforward",
    "backward_compatibility": "Fix requires changing DB call semantics (atomic decrement, transactions) which may require schema or query changes",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No logging or metrics on stock check failures, race condition occurrences, or partial failures — add observability before deploying",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": false,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": []
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Revert function to previous version; no persistent state changes beyond the bugs described"
  },
  "escalations": [],
  "confidence": {
    "level": 97,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Race condition between getStock and decrementStock is textbook TOCTOU",
      "Lack of transaction boundary is evident from sequential awaits",
      "Negative quantity exploit follows directly from missing validation"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Exact DB driver behavior for decrementStock with negative values depends on implementation"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "decrementStock performs stock = stock - quantity without its own floor check",
      "db.cart.addItem and db.products.decrementStock are not already wrapped in an external transaction by the caller"
    ]
  }
}
